About.title: Who <gold>we are</gold>
About.intro: Sterkte Records was born from a passion for authentic music and a commitment to supporting artists in a constantly evolving industry.
About.visionText: Build a bridge between artists' creativity and a global audience through personalized strategies, optimal digital distribution, and transparent management. Founded in 2020, our label is built on innovation, excellence and closeness with our talents.
About.missionText: Support every artist, whatever their style or background, to reveal their potential and take them beyond borders. From composition to success, Sterkte Records guides you every step of the way.

axel.name: Axel l'or Kaumba
axel.description: Digital marketing expert with several years of industry experience. Passionate about discovering new talent.

abigail.name: Abigail Angelani
abigail.description: Specialist in digital strategies and innovative promotional campaigns.

diademe.name: Diadème Ngandu
diademe.description: Personalized support for artists on career development and project management.

Pricing.title: One simple price, built for your career
Pricing.description: One subscription, 100% of your royalties. No revenue share, no hidden tiers — just the price that gets you distributed everywhere.

solo.description: The reference plan to distribute your music worldwide.

features.unlimitedReleases: Unlimited releases, all formats (Single, EP, Album)
features.allDsps: 150+ platforms including Spotify, Apple Music, Deezer, Boomplay

africa.name: Africa
africa.description: The same Solo plan, priced for the purchasing power of select African markets.
africa.eligibilityNote: Reserved for eligible countries (DR Congo, Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Senegal and other African markets). Eligibility is determined automatically at signup based on your country.

label.name: Label
label.description: For signed artists and labels who want full support, managed directly by our team.
label.pricingNote: Custom quote — revenue split and terms are defined in your contract.
features: Dedicated support from our A&R team

Pricing.royaltiesNote: 100% of the royalties generated by your streams come back to you. Sterkte Records takes no commission on your DSP revenue — our business model relies on subscriptions, add-ons, and our services (studio, booking, consulting).

Faq.title: Frequently asked questions
Faq.description: Everything you need to know before distributing your music with Sterkte Records.

q1.question: Which audio file formats are accepted?
q1.answer: WAV and FLAC are recommended for maximum quality; MP3 is also accepted. A master of at least 16-bit / 44.1 kHz is required.

q2.question: How long does it take for my music to go live?
q2.answer: We submit your release to platforms within 5 to 7 business days after full validation. Release delays then vary by platform (3 to 14 business days). We recommend submitting at least 3 weeks before your desired release date.

q3.question: Do I keep the rights to my music?
q3.answer: Yes, 100%. Sterkte Records acts as a distribution agent: you remain the sole owner of your masters and copyrights.

q4.question: When do I get paid, and from what amount?
q4.answer: Royalties are paid out quarterly, within 30 days after the end of each quarter, from a minimum threshold of $25 (or equivalent).

q5.question: Which platforms will my music be available on?
q5.answer: Spotify, Apple Music, Deezer, YouTube Music, TikTok, Boomplay, Audiomack, Amazon Music, Tidal and many more — over 150 platforms in total.

q6.question: Can I take down my music at any time?
q6.answer: Yes, from your artist space or by email. The takedown is completed within 7 to 30 business days depending on the platforms involved.

q7.question: Do you support Mobile Money payments?
q7.answer: Yes, in addition to international credit cards, we support Mobile Money (Airtel Money, M-Pesa, Orange Money, MTN) for artists in Africa.

q8.question: How can I contact you with a question?
q8.answer: By email at contact.sterkterecords@gmail.com or via our contact form — we reply within 48 business hours.

Onboarding.title: Create your artist profile
Onboarding.subtitle: A few details to get started — you can change everything later.
Onboarding.bioPlaceholder: A few words about you and your music…
Onboarding.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

format.explanation: Platforms only accept WAV, FLAC, or MP3.
format.suggestion: Export your track in one of these formats and try again.

codec.message: This WAV file appears to be compressed, not uncompressed PCM.
codec.explanation: Platforms expect a standard PCM WAV file, without compression.
codec.suggestion: Re-export your file as "WAV PCM" (sometimes called "standard WAV") from your audio software.

sampleRate.explanation: 44,100 Hz (CD quality) is the minimum recommended by platforms.
sampleRate.suggestion: Re-export your track at 44,100 Hz or higher if your software allows it.

bitDepth.explanation: 16 bits is the minimum recommended for good audio quality.
bitDepth.suggestion: Re-export your track at 16 bits or higher (24 bits recommended for a master).

tooShort.explanation: A duration under one second usually means an incomplete or corrupted file.
tooShort.suggestion: Check that the right file was selected and try again.

silenceStart.message: There are {seconds} seconds of silence at the very start of the track.
silenceStart.explanation: A long silence at the start can disrupt playlist or shuffle listening.
silenceStart.suggestion: Trim the extra silence at the start of your file before re-uploading.

silenceEnd.message: There are {seconds} seconds of silence at the very end of the track.
silenceEnd.explanation: A long silence at the end can disrupt the transition to the next track.
silenceEnd.suggestion: Trim the extra silence at the end of your file before re-uploading.

clipping.message: Clipping detected on {percent}% of the signal.
clipping.explanation: Clipping happens when the volume exceeds the maximum possible level, damaging the sound.
clipping.suggestion: Lower the overall level slightly during mastering and re-export your track.

abnormalPeaks.explanation: An isolated peak much louder than the rest of the signal can be a click, a crackle, or a recording issue.
abnormalPeaks.suggestion: Listen back to your track at the affected moments to check for anything to fix.

tooQuiet.message: This track seems very quiet ({value} dBFS on average).
tooQuiet.explanation: A track that's too quiet can sound weaker than other tracks during playback.
tooQuiet.suggestion: Increase the overall level during mastering before re-uploading your track.

tooLoud.message: This track seems very loud ({value} dBFS on average).
tooLoud.explanation: A very high level leaves little headroom and increases the risk of distortion.
tooLoud.suggestion: Check your mastering: a bit more headroom avoids unpleasant surprises.

duplicateInBatch.message: This file has already been added to this release.
duplicateInBatch.explanation: Two identical tracks were detected in the same upload.
duplicateInBatch.suggestion: Remove one of the two duplicate files.

duplicateInCatalog.message: This file looks like a track already in your catalog.
duplicateInCatalog.explanation: The same audio file already exists on another one of your releases.
duplicateInCatalog.suggestion: Check that this isn't an accidental duplicate upload.

integrity.message: This file couldn't be read correctly.
integrity.explanation: The file appears to be corrupted or in a format not supported by the browser.
integrity.suggestion: Re-export your track from your audio software and try again.

durationConsistency.message: The duration declared in the file doesn't match the actual detected duration.
durationConsistency.explanation: This mismatch can indicate a truncated file or an incorrect header.
durationConsistency.suggestion: Re-export your track from your audio software and try again.

integrity.message: This image couldn't be read correctly.
integrity.explanation: The file appears to be corrupted or in an unsupported format.
integrity.suggestion: Re-export your cover art and try again.

format.explanation: Platforms accept JPEG or PNG files.
format.suggestion: Export your cover art as JPEG or PNG and try again.

dimensions.explanation: Platforms require a square cover of at least 3000×3000 pixels.
dimensions.suggestion: Use a larger image or resize your cover to at least 3000×3000 px.

ratio.explanation: The cover must have an exactly square ratio (width = height).
ratio.suggestion: Crop your image into a perfect square.

fileSize.message: This file is quite large ({megabytes} MB).
fileSize.explanation: A very heavy file can slow down upload and processing at the distributor.
fileSize.suggestion: Slightly compress your image with no visible quality loss before uploading.

colorProfile.message: This image appears to be in CMYK rather than RGB.
colorProfile.explanation: Platforms require an sRGB (RGB) color profile, not CMYK (used for printing).
colorProfile.suggestion: Convert your image to RGB/sRGB in your editing software before exporting.

overCompressed.message: This image appears to be heavily compressed.
overCompressed.explanation: Too much compression can create visible artifacts (blocks, blur) on the cover.
overCompressed.suggestion: Re-export your image at a higher JPEG quality (or as PNG).

blur.message: This image seems to lack sharpness.
blur.explanation: A blurry cover is less engaging and can look unprofessional.
blur.suggestion: Check the sharpness of your original artwork before exporting again.

empty.message: This image appears to be empty or flat.
empty.explanation: The image seems to contain almost no visual detail (solid color).
empty.suggestion: Check that the right file was selected.

margins.message: This image appears to have white or transparent margins.
margins.explanation: Platforms expect artwork that fills the entire square frame, without a border.
margins.suggestion: Crop your artwork so it fills the whole image area.

releaseTitle.suggestion: Enter a title for your release.

genre.suggestion: Choose a genre for your release.

language.suggestion: Enter the main language of your release.

trackTitle.message: The title of track {position} is required.
trackTitle.suggestion: Enter a title for this track.

mainArtistConsistency.message: No main artist is set for track {position}.
mainArtistConsistency.explanation: Each track must have at least one contributor with the "Main artist" role.
mainArtistConsistency.suggestion: Add a contributor with the "Main artist" role for this track.

featuringConsistency.message: The title of track {position} mentions a featuring that isn't declared.
featuringConsistency.explanation: The title contains "feat." or "ft." but no "Featuring" contributor has been added.
featuringConsistency.suggestion: Add the featured artist to this track's contributors.

explicitConsistency.message: At least one track is marked "explicit content" but the release isn't.
explicitConsistency.explanation: If a track contains explicit language, the whole release must also be marked as such.
explicitConsistency.suggestion: Check "Explicit content" at the release level.

dateConsistency.message: The recording date is after the release date.
dateConsistency.explanation: A recording can't have happened after the track was released.
dateConsistency.suggestion: Check and correct one of the two dates.

isrcFormat.message: The ISRC "{value}" for track {position} isn't in the right format.
isrcFormat.explanation: A valid ISRC has 12 characters: 2 country letters, 3 characters, then 7 digits (e.g. FRXXX2400001).
isrcFormat.suggestion: Fix the ISRC, or leave the field empty so one is generated automatically.

upcFormat.explanation: A valid UPC has 12 digits with a correct check digit.
upcFormat.suggestion: Fix the UPC code, or leave the field empty so one is generated automatically.

splitsSum.message: The rights split for track {position} totals {sum}% instead of 100%.
splitsSum.explanation: The sum of all contributors' percentages must be exactly 100%.
splitsSum.suggestion: Adjust the percentages so they total 100%.

duplicateIsrc.message: The ISRC for track {position} is already used in your catalog.
duplicateIsrc.explanation: Each track must have a unique ISRC.
duplicateIsrc.suggestion: Leave the ISRC field empty so a new code is generated automatically.

duplicateUpc.message: This UPC code is already used by another one of your releases.
duplicateUpc.explanation: Each release must have a unique UPC.
duplicateUpc.suggestion: Leave the UPC field empty so a new code is generated automatically.

duplicateTitle.message: A release with this title already exists in your catalog.
duplicateTitle.explanation: Two releases with the same title can be confusing for your listeners.
duplicateTitle.suggestion: Check this isn't an accidental duplicate, or make the title more specific (e.g. "Title (Remix)").
